complaints:
A Biopsy(Right Post Temple) 
Dx: BASAL CELL CARCINOMA, INFILTRATIVE
procedure:
SRT Delivery (SRT Tx)
Quantity: 1
SITE:   Nose
Field Number:3
Prescription:1
Fractionation Number: 9
Energy:   100  kV
Treatment time:0.45 minutes
Dose Per Treatment: 272.70 cGy
Cumulative Dose: 2454.3 cGy
Radiation Therapy Field (SRT Field)
Quantity: 1
Physician Orders: Radiotherapy Treatment Plan: Superficial Radiotherapy with Ultrasound
Plan: This patient has been treated today with image-guided superficial radiation therapy for non-melanoma skin cancer. Written informed consent has been previously obtained from this patient for this treatment. This consent is documented in the patient's chart. The patient gave verbal consent to continue treatment today. The patient was treated with a specific radiation dose and setup as prescribed by the provider listed on this visit note. A Radiation Therapist performed administration of radiation under the supervision of a provider. The treatment parameters and cumulative dose are indicated above. Prior to administering the radiation, the patient underwent a verification therapeutic radiology simulation-aided field setting defining relevant normal and abnormal target anatomy and acquiring images with a separate and distinct diagnostic high-frequency ultrasound to delineate tissues and determine whether to proceed with delivery of therapeutic, in addition to retrieve data necessary to develop an optimal radiation treatment process for the patient. The field placement simulation documents any change seen in overall tumor volume documented in the patient’s record, allows the clinician to indicate any needed changes in the treatment plan and/or prescription, provides diagnostic evaluation as the basis for performing the therapeutic procedure, and clearly identifies the information needed to decide to proceed with the therapeutic procedure.
This process includes verification of the treatment port(s) and proper treatment positioning. All treatment ports were photographed within electronic medical records. The patient's lead blocking along with gross tumor volume and margin was confirmed. Considering superficial radiotherapy is clinical in setup, this requires the physician and radiation therapist to clarify the location interest being treated against initial images, ultrasound, pathology, and patient anatomy. Care was taken to ensure fields treated were geometrically accurate and properly positioned using therapeutic radiology simulation-aided field setting verification per fraction. This process is also utilized to determine if any prescription or setup changes are necessary. These steps are therefore medically necessary to ensure safe and effective administration of radiation. Ongoing therapeutic radiology simulation-aided field setting verification is ordered throughout the course of therapy.
A high-frequency ultrasound image was acquired today for a two-dimensional evaluation of the tumor volume, depth, width, breadth, review of prior response to treatment, provide geometric accuracy of field placement, and determine whether to proceed with therapeutic delivery.
The field placement and ultrasound imaging, per fraction, is separate and distinct from the initial simulation and is an important task in providing safe administration of superficial radiation therapy. Physician evaluation of the ultrasound delivery will be ongoing throughout the course of treatment and is deemed medically necessary to ensure the efficacy of treatment, whether to proceed with therapeutic delivery, and determine any necessary changes. Today's images were evaluated for determination of continuation of treatment with the current plan or with necessary changes as appropriate. According to the review of verification therapeutic radiology simulation-aided field setting and imaging, no change is required. Additionally, the use of ultrasound visualization and targeted assessment allows the patient to be able to see their cancer progress, encouraging the patient to complete and maintain compliance through the full course of prescribed radiotherapy. Per Dr. RAO, continued ultrasound guidance and therapeutic radiology simulation-aided field setting verification per fraction is required for field placement, measurement of tumor depth, tissues evaluation, progress, acute effect monitoring, and determination for therapeutic treatment delivery is appropriate.
Ultrasound Evaluation - Ultrasound Guidance (US)
Quantity: 1
High frequency ultrasound depth is 1.65  mm, which is 0.16 mm in difference from previous image.

patientSummary: 92 years old Male patient, Paul Atkinson was seen on 04-08-2024 as a follow up patient.

complaints: A Biopsy(Right Nose)     Dx: SQUAMOUS CELL CARCINOMA, IN SITU
procedure:
Per the request of Dr. Rao, continuing medical physics review as per radiotherapy standard of care post every 5th fraction for patient, including assessment of treatment parameters, quality assurance of dose delivery, and review of patient treatment documentation in support of the provider, has been completed for fractions 7-12, ensuring efficacy and continued safe delivery of radiotherapy. Included in physics check is review of patient setup information, all pertinent simulation and treatment photographs checks, prescription, dose calculation verification, daily dose charted correctly, elapsed days and treatment days correctly charted, cumulative dose correct, and review of any prescription changes. Patient was not present, nor was it necessary for the patient to be present for weekly physics review and no other superficial radiotherapy services were rendered on this day. Continued medical physics review post every 5th fraction of therapy is requested by provider for appropriate radiotherapy management and is deemed medically necessary and standard of care.
Radiation Physics Consult Weekly (SRT Physic)
Location: right nasal dorsum
Quantity: 1

Physician Orders: Plan: On Treatment Visit (OTV) with Ultrasound
Plan: The patient is undergoing superficial radiation therapy for skin cancer and presents for weekly evaluation and management. Per protocol and as documented on the flow sheet, the patient was questioned as to subjective redness, pruritus, pain, drainage, fatigue, or any other symptoms. Objectively, the radiation area was evaluated with regards to erythema, atrophy, scale, crusting, erosion, ulceration, edema, purpura, tenderness, warmth, drainage, and any other findings. The plan was extensively reviewed including dose and dosing schedule. The simulation and clinical setup were also reviewed as were external and any internal shields and based on this review the appropriateness and sufficiency of treatment was determined.
A high-frequency ultrasound image was acquired today for a two-dimensional evaluation of the tumor volume, depth, width, breadth, review of prior response to treatment, provide geometric accuracy of field placement, and determine whether to proceed with therapeutic delivery.
Per Dr. Rao , continued ultrasound guidance and therapeutic radiology simulation-aided field setting
verification per fraction is required for field placement, measurement of tumor depth, tissues evaluation, progress, acute effect monitoring, and determination for therapeutic treatment delivery is appropriate.

patientSummary: 62 years old Female patient, Angie Lambrou was seen on 03-04-2026 as a follow up patient.

complaints:
On 12/15//2025, patient is present presents to discuss SRT/ Patient is from Visalia and was diagnosed with BCC to the nose by peak dermatology and would like to discuss SRT as a treatment option. The signs and symptoms include erythematous, pearly telangiectatic papule located on the nose. The biopsy was taken on 11/18/2025.  Positive results reported back as Basal Cell Carcinoma superficial and nodular.  The problem has not been preciously treated. The quality of the lesion is erythematous, pearly telangiectatic papule.  The patient was presented with the following treatment options: IGSRT and Mohs surgery.  Image-guided superficial radiation therapy was chosen over other treatment options presented due to NMSC, low risk, advanced age, tumor location, and patient refused surgery.
The patient had a complete consultation regarding applicable modalities for treatment. After discussing all risks, benefits, and alternatives and based on a variety of factors (as detailed in the history above), it was determined that the patient would begin superficial radiation therapy.
assesment: Patient presents for the Image Guided Superficial Radiation Therapy (IGSRT) Treatment of the Left Nasal Ala
procedure:
Comment:  Nothing new to report
 
Image Guided Superficial Radiation Therapy (IGSRT) Fraction #2
Physician Orders: Radiotherapy Treatment Plan - Superficial Radiotherapy with Ultrasound
Radiation Therapy Field (SRT Field)
Quantity: 1
Plan: This patient has been treated today with image-guided superficial radiation therapy for non-melanoma skin cancer. Written informed consent has been previously obtained from this patient for this treatment. This consent is documented in the patient's chart. The patient gave verbal consent to continue treatment today. The patient was treated with a specific radiation dose and setup as prescribed by the provider listed on this visit note. A Radiation Therapist performed administration of radiation under the supervision of a provider. The treatment parameters and cumulative dose are indicated above. Prior to administering the radiation, the patient underwent a verification therapeutic radiology simulation-aided field setting defining relevant normal and abnormal target anatomy and acquiring images with a separate and distinct diagnostic high-frequency ultrasound to delineate tissues and determine whether to proceed with delivery of therapeutic radiotherapy, in addition to retrieving data necessary to develop an optimal radiation treatment process for the patient. The field placement simulation documents any change seen in overall tumor volume documented in the patient’s record, allows the clinician to indicate any needed changes in the treatment plan and/or prescription, provides diagnostic evaluation as the basis for performing the therapeutic procedure, and clearly identifies the information needed to decide to proceed with the therapeutic procedure.
This process includes verification of the treatment port(s) and proper treatment positioning. All treatment ports were photographed within electronic medical records. The patient's lead blocking along with gross tumor volume and margin was confirmed. Considering superficial radiotherapy is clinical in setup, this requires the physician and radiation therapist to clarify the location interest being treated against initial images, ultrasound, pathology, and patient anatomy. Care was taken to ensure fields treated were geometrically accurate and properly positioned using therapeutic radiology simulation-aided field setting verification per fraction. This process is also utilized to determine if any prescription or setup changes are necessary. These steps are therefore medically necessary to ensure safe and effective administration of radiation. Ongoing therapeutic radiology simulation-aided field setting verification is ordered throughout the course of therapy.
A high-frequency ultrasound image was acquired today for a two-dimensional evaluation of the tumor volume, depth, width, breadth, review of prior response to treatment, provide geometric accuracy of field placement, and determine whether to proceed with therapeutic delivery.
The field placement and ultrasound imaging, per fraction, is separate and distinct from the initial simulation and is an important task in providing safe administration of superficial radiation therapy. Physician evaluation of the ultrasound delivery will be ongoing throughout the course of treatment and is deemed medically necessary to ensure the efficacy of treatment, whether to proceed with therapeutic delivery, and determine any necessary changes. Today's images were evaluated for determination of continuation of treatment with the current plan or with necessary changes as appropriate. According to the review of verification therapeutic radiology simulation-aided field setting and imaging, no change is required. Additionally, the use of ultrasound visualization and targeted assessment allows the patient to be able to see their cancer progress, encouraging the patient to complete and maintain compliance through the full course of prescribed radiotherapy.
Per Dr. Rao, continued ultrasound guidance and therapeutic radiology simulation-aided field setting verification per fraction is required for field placement, measurement of tumor depth, tissues evaluation, progress, acute effect monitoring, and determination for therapeutic treatment delivery is appropriate.
